prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "髂腰肌弓步拉伸" (kneeling lunge hip flexor stretch) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person in a kneeling lunge hip flexor stretch position: front leg (right) bent with foot flat on the ground and knee at 90 degrees, knee directly over ankle. Rear leg (left) kneeling with the knee on the ground (on a soft mat) and the top of the foot pointing back. Hips pushed forward to feel the stretch in the front of the rear (left) hip and thigh. Torso completely upright (vertical), or both arms raised straight overhead with hands together to deepen the hip flexor stretch. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: the rear LEFT KNEE is on the ground, the front RIGHT KNEE is over the right ankle at 90 degrees.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: hip flexors (psoas major and rectus femoris) of the rear (left) leg under stretch. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: quadriceps of the rear leg and gluteus maximus of the front leg. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "髂腰肌" pointing to the iliopsoas (hip flexor) of the rear leg; "股直肌" pointing to the rectus femoris of the rear leg; "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus of the front leg. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
